title: Belichick: Tom Brady leaving Patriots in 2020 was ‘absolutely the right thing for him to do’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick stated Tom Brady made the right decision leaving the Patriots in 2020 for the Buccaneers.
After Brady's departure, the Patriots went 29-38 over four seasons with one playoff appearance before Belichick's exit.
Brady won Super Bowl LV with Tampa and completed 66.7% of passes for 14,643 yards, 108 touchdowns in three seasons.

title: Hot dogs in historic paintings? Kayem and the MFA imagine exactly that.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kayem Foods and the Museum of Fine Arts Boston created an art series adding hot dogs to historic American scenes.
The series, "There When It Mattered Most," premieres June 4 at Faneuil Hall and tours New England this summer.
Kayem launches a limited-edition Cannon Fire Hot Dog, sold for $2.50, alongside the campaign.

title: Patriots’ Brenden Schooler shares his opinion on grass vs. turf as Gillette Stadium undergoes changes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Gillette Stadium converted from artificial turf to natural grass for the 2026 FIFA World Cup to meet FIFA regulations.
Patriots player Brenden Schooler prefers playing on grass due to fewer injuries and better joint comfort compared to turf.
NFLPA Executive Director JC Tretter says 92% of surveyed NFL players prefer grass over turf for better safety and comfort.

title: A man died after getting stuck at the bottom of an MBTA escalator. Authorities are still investigating.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Steven McCluskey died after his clothing became trapped in an escalator at Davis Station on February 27.
The escalator wasn't stopped until more than 20 minutes after McCluskey fell, according to footage obtained by The Boston Globe.
The Middlesex County District Attorney’s Office is investigating the incident as an open case to determine the cause and manner of death.

title: ‘Immature’ and ‘out of control’: How an inside baseball feud is roiling the Boston City Council
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Councilors Erin Murphy and Sharon Durkan are clashing over procedural moves to block late-filed measures.
Durkan blocks late-files citing open meeting law, while Murphy claims it's an obstruction of council work.
Both councilors accuse each other of immature behavior as budget season heightens tensions within the council.

title: Belmont hair salon gutted by fire that also destroyed ice cream shop
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A fire gutted Lucy Danger Hair salon in Belmont and destroyed Moozy’s Ice Cream Emporium last Thursday.
Salon owner Lucy Ankner, on vacation during the fire, decided to leave the salon industry for a new career path.
A GoFundMe was launched to help stylists Sarah Paul Migliozzi and Theresa Curran replace tools and recover income.

title: Massport is launching a first-of-its-kind remote terminal for Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massport will open a remote terminal in Framingham for Logan Airport starting June 1.
Initially, JetBlue and Delta passengers will use the service, with plans to include more airlines.
The cost is $9 each way, with secure bus service from 5:30 a.m. to 4 p.m.

title: These spray decks have opened to deal with the record heat
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston and Massachusetts opened over a dozen spray decks amid record-breaking heat, officials confirmed.
A heat advisory is active in southern New England, with expected highs of 94 degrees Tuesday.
14 spray decks, managed by DCR, became operational in cities like Boston, Cambridge, and Revere.

title: New City Microcreamery expands to Chestnut Hill near Boston College
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: New City Microcreamery opens at 19 Commonwealth Ave, Chestnut Hill, replacing the former White Mountain Creamery location.
The ice cream shop offers a unique 'Cookie Monster' flavor with sweet cream, cookie dough, Oreo pieces, and brownie bites.
New City has other locations in Hudson, Sudbury, Cambridge, Arlington, and Bolton, using liquid nitrogen for a smoother texture.

title: Kentucky man arrested in Mass. gun store robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Aaron Woodrum, 33, was arrested in a Norwood gun store break-in after surveillance identified him as the suspect.
Police found stolen firearms near Neponset Street and linked Woodrum to the robbery after responding to a hotel incident.
Woodrum faces multiple felony charges and remains in custody after failing to post a $100,000 bail.
